activities_projects.title: Compare and Contrast Chart
activities_projects.objective: Compare the themes and characters of both stories.
steps: Draw two columns for ‘His First Flight’ and ‘The Black Aeroplane’
List similarities and differences in theme, setting, and characters
Present findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop comparative analysis skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of comparison, relevant points, and presentation

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand how events lead to consequences in both stories.
steps: Identify 3 major events in each story
Write their causes and effects
Discuss how these events change the characters
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will strengthen analytical thinking skills.

activities_projects.title: Diary Entry – Young Seagull
steps: Imagine you are the young seagull
Write a diary entry before and after your first flight
Highlight the change in emotions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character development and express creatively.

activities_projects.title: Mystery Discussion – The Black Aeroplane
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the mysterious elements of the story.
steps: Discuss who the unknown pilot could be
Write two possible explanations
Support your answers with reasons from the text
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop interpretation and reasoning skills.

activities_projects.title: Theme Exploration Poster
activities_projects.objective: Identify and explain key themes such as courage and hope.
steps: Select one theme from each story
Write examples supporting the theme
Add illustrations to represent the idea
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect themes with textual evidence.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct theme identification and supporting examples

activities_projects.title: Speech on Overcoming Fear
activities_projects.objective: Relate story message to real life.
steps: Prepare a 2-minute speech on overcoming fear
Include examples from the seagull’s story
Present confidently in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop public speaking and application skills.

activities_projects.title: Character Comparison Activity
activities_projects.objective: Compare the young seagull and the pilot.
steps: List qualities of both characters
Identify how each shows courage differently
Write a short paragraph comparing them
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand different expressions of courage.

activities_projects.title: Alternate Ending Writing
steps: Imagine the seagull never flew
Write an alternate ending explaining consequences
Share with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance imagination and narrative writing.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary in Context Task
steps: Choose 8 new words from the chapter
Write meanings
Use them in a short paragraph about flying
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will use vocabulary correctly in context.

activities_projects.title: Newspaper Report – Safe Landing
steps: Imagine you are a reporter at the airport
Write a news report about the pilot’s safe landing
Include headline, date, and key details
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will learn structured formal writing.
